isolation.approach: Extracted validation logic from main.js and tested in isolation via Node.js scripts

steps.action: Read main.js and extract URL validation logic
steps.expected: Validation uses new URL() constructor and checks protocol against ['https:', 'http:']
steps.observed: Confirmed: main.js checks typeof url, parses with new URL(), and validates protocol whitelist
Returns { success: false, error: 'Protocol not allowed: file:' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Protocol not allowed: javascript:' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Protocol not allowed: ftp:' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Protocol not allowed: data:' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid URL: Invalid URL' }

steps.action: Test non-string inputs (number, null, undefined, object)
steps.observed: All return { success: false, error: 'Invalid URL: expected a string' }
Returns { success: true }
Returns { success: true }

evidence.codeVerification: main.js contains: typeof url check, new URL(url), ['https:', 'http:'] protocol whitelist

steps.action: Read main.js and extract path validation logic
steps.observed: Confirmed: main.js validates filename type/non-empty, resolves full path with path.resolve(), and checks startsWith with path.sep
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid file path: path traversal detected' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid file path: path traversal detected' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid file path: path traversal detected' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid filename' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid filename' }
Returns { success: true } for video1.mp4, subdir/file.txt, CCNA_Lab_File.pdf
Confirmed: '%2e%2e/etc/passwd' stays literal inside resources dir. File won't exist at that literal path — defense-in-depth via fs.promises.access()

evidence.codeVerification: main.js contains: typeof filename check, path.resolve(), startsWith(resourcesPath + path.sep)
evidence.notes: path.join on Linux joins '/base' + '/etc/passwd' as '/base/etc/passwd' (correct behavior). %2e%2e not decoded by path.resolve.

steps.action: Read main.js and extract videoId validation logic
steps.observed: Confirmed: main.js checks typeof videoId and validates with /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{11}$/.test(videoId)
Returns { success: true }

steps.action: Test valid 11-char with underscore and hyphen
steps.observed: Returns { success: true } for 'abc_def-ghi'
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid video ID: must be 11 alphanumeric characters' }
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid video ID: must be 11 alphanumeric characters' }
Returns { success: false } for '!' character
Returns { success: false }
Returns { success: false } for '<script>alert(1)</script>'

steps.action: Test null, numeric, undefined, full URL
steps.observed: All return { success: false }

steps.action: Read main.js and extract windowId validation logic
steps.observed: Confirmed: main.js checks typeof windowId !== 'number'
Returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid window ID: must be a number' }
Returns { success: false }
Returns { success: false }
Returns { success: false }
Returns { success: false }
Returns { success: false }
All return { success: true }

evidence.notes: NaN passes typeof check (typeof NaN === 'number') but has no security impact — the window simply won't be found in the Map

assertions.title: All IPC validation failures return structured error objects

steps.action: Collect all rejection results from VAL-IPC-001 through VAL-IPC-004
steps.observed: 21 rejection cases collected from all 4 handlers

steps.action: Verify all rejections have success: false
steps.observed: All 21 cases confirmed success: false

steps.action: Verify all rejections have non-empty string error
steps.expected: All 21 cases have typeof error === 'string' && error.length > 0
steps.observed: All 21 cases confirmed non-empty descriptive error strings

steps.action: Verify no exceptions thrown by any validation
steps.expected: All validation functions handle errors gracefully
steps.observed: 10 exception-safety tests all passed — no exceptions thrown

evidence.codeVerification: main.js consistently uses { success: false, error: <string> } pattern across all IPC handlers

frictions.description: Could not invoke IPC handlers directly via ipcRenderer.invoke since Electron cannot be fully started in headless mode. Validation logic had to be extracted and tested in isolation.
frictions.resolution: Extracted the exact validation code from main.js into standalone Node.js functions and tested them. Code verification (grep for patterns in main.js) confirmed the validation exists in the actual codebase.

summary: All 5 IPC validation assertions (VAL-IPC-001 through VAL-IPC-005) passed. 56/56 individual test cases passed covering URL protocol validation, path traversal prevention, videoId format validation, windowId type checking, and structured error object consistency. Validation logic was extracted from main.js and tested in isolation since Electron IPC handlers cannot be invoked without a running Electron instance. Code verification confirmed all validation patterns exist in the actual main.js source.
